answer:
Verdict: migrate to managed (EKS/GKE) ASAP + hire 1 replacement in parallel. Don't rely on contractors alone.

Reasoning:
- Self-managed k8s w/ 40 services + 10 eng = bus factor 1, just proven. Replacing 1 hero rebuilds same fragility.
- Managed control plane kills etcd, upgrades, cert rotation, HA master toil — the stuff needing deep specialist. Migration usually 4–8 wks for this size if manifests portable.
- 10-person team can't justify dedicated platform team; managed + IaC lets 2–3 senior devs share ops.

Plan:
1. Now: short-term contractor/firm (2–3 mo) to keep lights on + execute migration. Contractors fine for project work, bad as permanent ops owners (knowledge walks out, incident response gaps).
2. Weeks 1–2: audit what quitter owned — CI/CD, secrets, DNS, backups, runbooks. Rotate creds.
3. Mo 1–3: migrate to EKS/GKE, Terraform everything, managed add-ons (RDS/CloudSQL if self-hosting DBs too).
4. Hire 1 platform/SRE eng anyway — managed ≠ zero ops (cost, observability, CI/CD, security). Hiring takes 2–4 mo, start now.

Cost: managed control plane ~$70–75/mo/cluster, trivial vs 1 FTE ($150–200k). Contractor bridge ~$20–40k total.

Skip if: heavy on-prem/compliance constraints forcing self-managed — then hire 2, not 1, never single-point again.
